name: USS Bismark Sea (CVE 95)
events: 21 Oct 1944 From 21 to 23 October 1944, USS Archerfish (T/Cdr. J.F. Enright, USN), USS Kete (T/Cdr. R.L. Rutter, USN) and USS Sea Cat (T/Cdr. R.R. McGregor, USN) conducted exercises off Pearl Harbor with an inbound convoy coming from San Diego. This convoy was made up of the escort carriers USS Makin Island (T/Capt. W.B. Whaley, Jr., USN), USS Lunga Point (T/Capt. G.A.T. Washburn, USN), USS Bismarck Sea (T/Capt. J.L. Pratt, USN) and USS Salamaua (T/Capt. J.I. Taylor, Jr., USN). They were escorted since departure from San Diego by the destroyer escort USS Tills (Lt.Cdr. J.L. Brooks, USNR). The escort had been reinforced by the destroyer escorts USS Connolly (Lt.Cdr. W.A. Collier, USNR) and USS Bright (Lt.Cdr. W.A. McMahan, USNR) which had escorted the three submarines from Pearl Harbor to the rendezvous point with the incoming convoy.

21 Feb 1945 The Bismarck Sea was attacked by 3 Japanese kamikazes, two of which were shot down. The other plane crashed onto her deck, exploding her ammunition storage, causing uncontrollable fires, despite the efforts of her crew. The carrier took 90 minutes to sink with 318 men lost. (1)
